LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FRANKLY, INC, STEVE CHUNG, LOUIS SCHWARTZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. BOTH DEMURRERS AND THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION ARE OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANTS TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THERE WILL BE NO REQUIREMENT TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT UNTIL THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OCCURS. THE CASE IS STAYED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS UNTIL THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS RESOLVED. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Jay Lekfowitz, Esq., Nathaniel Kritzer, Esq. Michael Esser, Esq. 415-439-1973 of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for Defendants Frankly, Chung, Schwartz.

DEFENDANT FRANKLY, INC, STEVE CHUNG, LOUIS SCHWARTZ's VERIFIED APPLICATION OF NATHANIEL J. KRITZER TO APPEAR PRO HAC VICE.

Defendants Frankly, Inc., Steve Chung, and Louis Schwartz's application for the pro hac vice admission of Nathaniel Kritzer is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cody Harris, Esq. 415-391-5400 of Keker Van Nest & Peters for Plaintiff.
Jay Lekfowitz, Esq., Nathaniel Kritzer, Esq. Michael Esser, Esq. 415-439-1973 of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for Defendants Frankly, Chung, Schwartz.
Charles Jaeger, Esq. 415-962-2879 of Farmer Brownstein Jaeger & Goldstein LLP for Defenda SKP America LLP

RULING - A R G U E D. The Pro Hac Vice motions are uncontested. The Court shares his views re: Demurrers and motion to compel arbitration. After arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANKLY, INC, STEVE CHUNG, LOUIS SCHWARTZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Both Demurrers and the alternative motion to compel arbitration are Off calendar. Defendants to file a motion to compel arbitration. There will be no requirement to respond to the complaint until the motion to compel arbitration occurs. The case is stayed in all other respects until the motion to compel arbitration is resolved.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cody Harris, Esq. 415-391-5400 of Keker Van Nest & Peters for Plaintiff.
Jay Lekfowitz, Esq., Nathaniel Kritzer, Esq. Michael Esser, Esq. 415-439-1973 of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for Defendants Frankly, Chung, Schwartz.
Charles Jaeger, Esq. 415-962-2879 of Farmer Brownstein Jaeger & Goldstein LLP for Defenda SKP America LLP

RULING - A R G U E D. The Pro Hac Vice motions are uncontested. The Court shares his views re: Demurrers and motion to compel arbitration. After arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Louis Schwartz's motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is denied. Plaintiffs satisfied their burden to show that this court has personal jurisdiction over Mr. Schwartz per specific jurisdiction principles. Mr. Schwartz purposefully availed himself of the benefits of California and his California contacts relate to plaintiffs' fraud claims. Plaintiffs presented evidence that Mr. Schwartz traveled to California for a meeting with Mr. Chung to discuss Frankly, Inc.'s acquisition of WorldNow Media, forwarded Mr. Chung's emails to plaintiffs, and sent WorldNow employees to California to facilitate the acquisition. Mr. Schwartz also received benefits from and engaged in significant activities in California in his capacity as CEO of Frankly Media, which, until recently, was headquartered in San Francisco. Mr. Schwartz has not shown that requiring him to defend this action in California is unreasonable.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
